filed.title: [REVIEW] Cross-Origin Policies: scored by presence only — `unsafe-none` / `cross-origin` get full credit
filed.finding: checkCrossOriginPolicies in src/rules.ts:145-164 scores COOP/COEP/CORP by presence only, awarding full 5/5 'good' status to the most permissive opt-out values (COOP: unsafe-none, COEP: unsafe-none, CORP: cross-origin) with no findings or recommendations.

filed.title: [REVIEW] CSP: missing `form-action` directive not flagged — `default-src 'self'` leaves form submissions unrestricted
filed.finding: checkCSP in src/rules.ts:41-71 never checks whether form-action is present. form-action does not fall back to default-src (same class of bug as closed #5 for base-uri). A policy of default-src 'self' awards 20/30 'good' with zero findings about form-action, leaving form submissions completely unrestricted.

runner_ups.finding: CSP wildcard regex /(?:default-src|script-src)\s+\*/i only matches when '*' is the first source token; 'default-src 'self' *' is not flagged.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; now covered by open issue #16

runner_ups.finding: checkCSP does not recognize Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header; report-only deployments are treated as if no CSP exists.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; needs design discussion on whether report-only should count for points

runner_ups.finding: checkCSP maxScore is 30 but the function can only award up to 20 points (starts at 20, only deductions). Developers see score: 20, maxScore: 30 for a perfect CSP with no way to earn the remaining 10 points.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score than filed finding; relates to future bonus-point criteria not yet implemented

runner_ups.finding: X-Frame-Options ALLOW-FROM value gets 8/15 and 'warning' status, but ALLOW-FROM is deprecated and ignored by all modern browsers — the tool implies partial protection where none exists.
HSTS check awards full credit (preload bonus) when 'preload' directive is present even if max-age < 63072000 (2 years), which is the minimum the actual hstspreload.org submission requires.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; smaller real-world consequence (sites are not auto-added to the preload list by the header alone)

runner_ups.finding: Referrer-Policy classifies 'no-referrer-when-downgrade' as a strong value (score 10), but it is the historical default and is widely considered weak for cross-origin URL leakage.
fetchHeaders uses method: 'HEAD' which causes false-missing readings on sites that set security headers conditionally on GET (e.g., HTML-only middleware).
checkCSP does not flag missing base-uri directive, leaving <base> injection silently bypassing script-src 'self'.

clean_areas: package.json exports/main/types/bin fields are correctly configured
CI workflows (ci.yml, publish.yml, auto-tag.yml) are sound
X-Frame-Options check (rules.ts:73-91) — accepts DENY/SAMEORIGIN and CSP frame-ancestors fallback as expected
PRs #9 and #10 (commits 8133c48, 5236522) — only changed .claude state files, no source changes
